The author mentions that they stopped investing in Defense EVs because Scarf Water Urshifu is no longer common. They considered 204 Defense EVs to survive Choice Band Surging Strikes, but decided the extra firepower was more valuable. This is the team's main sweeper. The author mentions that with the addition of Dragonite, Calyrex Shadow Rider can be used as a lead to weaken opponents. **Pokémon 2: Zamazenta** - Ability: Dauntless Shield - Held Item: Rusted Shield - Tera Type: Water - Nature: Jolly - Moves: Body Press / Heavy Slam / Wide Guard / Protect - EV Spread: 252 4 156 0 68 28 - EV Explanation: 252 HP EVs and 4 Attack EVs. 156 Defense EVs to survive Calyrex Shadow Rider's +2 Choice Specs Psychic 93.6% of the time. 68 Special Defense EVs. 28 Speed EVs to outspeed max Speed Terapagos by +2. The author mentions that reducing the Speed and Special Defense investment was a mistake, and that they should have invested more in Speed to win Zamazenta mirror matches. They also mention that reducing Defense investment made it less reliable to KO Water Urshifu with Body Press + Sucker Punch. **Pokémon 3: Chien-Pao** - Ability: Sword of Ruin - Held Item: Assault Vest - Tera Type: Water - Nature: Jolly - Moves: Ruination / Ice Spinner / Sucker Punch / Ice Shard - EV Spread: 188 0 0 0 196 124 - EV Explanation: 124 Speed EVs to outspeed準速 135 base Speed Pokemon (Miraidon and Chien-Pao). 188 HP EVs. 196 Special Defense EVs to survive Jolly Zacian's Play Rough 93.6% of the time when Terastallized into Water-type, and to survive Modest Miraidon's Electric Tera-boosted Dragon Energy. They also mention that the Assault Vest Chien-Pao was more effective because opponents were less likely to expect it. With the addition of Dragonite, Chien-Pao is often used as a switch-in when Calyrex Shadow Rider is used as a lead. The Water Tera type is used in matchups against Tornadus + Zacian or Water Urshifu where it is important to maintain a lead. **Pokémon 4: Dragonite** - Ability: Inner Focus - Held Item: Choice Band - Tera Type: Normal - Nature: Adamant - Moves: Extreme Speed / Low Kick / Rock Slide / Outrage - EV Spread: 244 252 4 0 4 4 - EV Explanation: 252 Attack EVs. 244 HP EVs to survive Modest Flutter Mane's Moonblast 93.6% of the time. The remaining EVs are split between Defense, Special Defense, and Speed. The author states that Dragonite is strong in the current meta. The moveset includes Extreme Speed, Low Kick to heavily damage Zacian, Zamazenta, and Calyrex Ice Rider, Rock Slide for coverage against Volcarona and Incineroar, and Outrage as a last resort. The Normal Tera type allows Dragonite to resist Fighting-type moves and makes Extreme Speed even more powerful. **Pokémon 5: Iron Valiant** - Ability: Quark Drive - Held Item: Booster Energy - Tera Type: Dark - Nature: Timid - Moves: Aura Sphere / Dazzling Gleam / Thunder Wave / Protect - EV Spread: 44 0 4 252 28 180 - EV Explanation: 252 Special Attack EVs. 180 Speed EVs to outspeed max Speed Iron Treads. 44 HP EVs. 4 Defense EVs. 28 Special Defense EVs to survive Timid Calyrex Shadow Rider's Astral Barrage. The author mentions that this slot is still being optimized. The moveset includes Aura Sphere for Zamazenta, Dazzling Gleam for coverage against Dark-type Calyrex Shadow Rider, and Thunder Wave to paralyze opponents. The author notes that they didn't use Thunder Wave much, and that Knock Off or Shadow Sneak might have been better options to provide more damage against Calyrex Shadow Rider. **Pokémon 6: Amoonguss** - Ability: Regenerator - Held Item: Mental Herb - Tera Type: Water - Nature: Relaxed - Moves: Sludge Bomb / Rage Powder / Spore / Protect - EV Spread: 236 0 220 0 52 0 - EV Explanation: 236 HP EVs. 220 Defense EVs to survive Adamant Chien-Pao's Ice Spinner 93.6% of the time. 52 Special Defense EVs to survive Modest Miraidon's Draco Meteor 93.6% of the time. The author mentions that this Pokémon's EV spread and moveset remained unchanged from the previous month. Dragonite and Iron Valiant were added to address specific threats and improve the team's overall versatility. https://liberty-note.com/2025/07/31/teru.summary: **TITLE: [Teru Fes x Viera Cup Winner] Calyrex Shadow Rider + Zamazenta** **Pokémon 1: Calyrex Shadow Rider** - Ability: As One (Spectrier) - Held Item: Focus Sash - Tera Type: Ghost - Nature: Timid - Moves: Astral Barrage / Psychic / Encore / Protect - EV Spread: 0 0 4 252 0 252 - EV Explanation: The EVs are invested to maximize Special Attack and Speed. The author considered investing in Defense to survive Choice Scarf Water Urshifu's attacks, but decided against it because reducing Special Attack would result in failing to KO certain threats. Encore is used to limit the opponent's Protect and Fake Out usage. The Tera Type is Ghost to increase the power of Astral Barrage. **Pokémon 2: Zamazenta** - Ability: Dauntless Shield - Held Item: Rusted Shield - Tera Type: Dragon - Nature: Impish - Moves: Body Press / Heavy Slam / Wide Guard / Protect - EV Spread: 220 0 156 0 0 132 - EV Explanation: Speed EVs are invested to outspeed max Speed Urshifu by 2 points. The remaining EVs are split between HP and Defense. The author wanted to avoid lowering HP too much due to Life Orb Calyrex Shadow Rider, so they lowered Defense instead. The Tera Type is Dragon to resist Water-type attacks from Kyogre. The author considered Grass and Water Tera Types, but Dragon was chosen because Grass has a weakness to Fire and Water has a weakness to Electric. **Pokémon 3: Chien-Pao** - Ability: Sword of Ruin - Held Item: Assault Vest - Tera Type: Poison - Nature: Jolly - Moves: Ice Spinner / Ruination / Sucker Punch / Ice Shard - EV Spread: 252 4 36 0 84 132 - EV Explanation: Speed EVs are invested to outspeed base 135 Speed Pokémon by 2 points. The remaining EVs are invested in HP and Special Defense. The author invested enough Defense to survive Urshifu's Water Tera-type Surging Strikes. The Tera Type is Poison to resist Fighting and Fairy-type attacks. The author suggests that a Ground Tera Type could be interesting to deal with Miraidon. **Pokémon 4: Chi-Yu** - Ability: Beads of Ruin - Held Item: Choice Scarf - Tera Type: Ghost - Nature: Modest - Moves: Heat Wave / Flamethrower / Overheat / Dark Pulse - EV Spread: 4 0 0 252 0 252 - EV Explanation: The EVs are invested to maximize Special Attack and Speed. The author felt that they did not need a Speed-boosting nature. The Tera Type is Ghost to avoid being targeted by Fake Out. **Pokémon 5: Amoonguss** - Ability: Regenerator - Held Item: Rocky Helmet - Tera Type: Water - Nature: Bold - Moves: Sludge Bomb / Spore / Rage Powder / Protect - EV Spread: 236 0 236 0 36 0 - EV Explanation: The EVs are invested heavily in HP and Defense to survive physical attacks. The author initially considered investing more in Special Defense, but decided to invest more in Defense to survive Guts Facade from Ursaluna. The Speed is set to be slower than the slowest Amoonguss and slower than Iron Ball Kyogre. The author chose Sludge Bomb over Pollen Puff because there are few opportunities to heal allies with Pollen Puff in this team. Sludge Bomb is useful against Fairy Tera Lunala and Grass Tera Pokémon. **Pokémon 6: Rillaboom** - Ability: Grassy Surge - Held Item: Seed Sower - Tera Type: Rock - Nature: Jolly - Moves: Grassy Glide / Wood Hammer / Follow Me / Needle Guard - EV Spread: 0 252 4 0 0 252 - EV Explanation: The EVs are invested to maximize Attack and Speed. The author considered investing in bulk to absorb more attacks, but decided against it because outspeeding Miraidon was too important. The Rillaboom can OHKO Miraidon with Ghost Tera Astral Barrage + Ivy Cudgel. The author chose Wood Hammer for the high damage output against Water Tera Calyrex Ice Rider. The combination of the ability and Follow Me makes the Rillaboom a reliable redirector. **CONCLUSION:** The team is built around Calyrex Shadow Rider and Zamazenta, with Chien-Pao providing support and disruption. Chi-Yu is included to improve the team's matchup against sun teams and provide additional offensive pressure. Amoonguss is used as a Trick Room counter and to provide support with Spore and Rage Powder. Rillaboom is included to deal with Volcarona and provide redirection support. **FINAL ARTICLE SUMMARY:** The article details a Pokémon Scarlet and Violet double battle team that won the "Teru Fes x Viera Cup". The team is built around Calyrex Shadow Rider and Zamazenta, with support from Chien-Pao, Chi-Yu, Amoonguss, and Rillaboom. - **Overall team strategy and concept**: The team aims to support Calyrex Shadow Rider with Zamazenta and other Pokémon, controlling the field and sweeping with Calyrex. - **Key Pokemon roles and synergies**: Calyrex Shadow Rider and Zamazenta form the core, with Chien-Pao providing offensive pressure and speed control, Chi-Yu countering sun teams, Amoonguss disrupting opponents, and Rillaboom redirecting attacks. - **TEAM STRENGTHS**: - The team has good coverage against common threats in the format. - The team is able to handle Trick Room teams. - The team has a strong matchup against sun teams. - The team has a reliable redirection support with Rillaboom. - **TEAM WEAKNESSES**: - The team can struggle against teams with strong priority moves. - The team can be vulnerable to setup sweepers. - **AUTHOR'S ASSESSMENT**: The author believes the team is well-rounded and capable of performing well in the current meta. - **Meta positioning and tournament viability**: The team is designed to counter common strategies and threats in the Regulation I format. - **Unique strategies or innovations mentioned**: The use of Assault Vest Chien-Pao is a notable choice, providing both offense and bulk. - **Author's recommendations and insights**: The author provides detailed explanations for the EV spreads, Tera Types, and move choices for each Pokémon. - **Team's competitive advantages and potential counters**: The team's strength lies in its ability to control the field and support Calyrex Shadow Rider.
